_model.note: Single source of truth for every Signal Lab creation. Edit THIS file, then run build_creations.py to regenerate creations.html. Content is decoupled from presentation: this same model feeds the library index, each creation hub, and (later) the visualizer + AI.

creation_fields.stage: seedling | growing | mature  (digital-garden growth, not a date)
creation_fields.audio: mp3 for playback (wav masters are not committed)
creation_fields.transformations: [{verb, feeling}] the DSP verbs as feelings
creation_fields.path: [{who, kind, text}] the creation dialogue — how it grew
creation_fields.inspiration: {title, credit, source_url, mood, thumb} a visual seed (e.g. an Unsplash photo). thumb stays local under inspiration/ (gitignored); we keep only credit + link in the repo.

creations.title: Vitrine
creations.tagline: The soul put through an ADC, and never fully recovered.
creations.source_material: A photograph of shop-window mannequins — stylized soulless souls; the digitized image of a person with the person gone. The middle figure is split down the seam by the glass: half in light, half swallowed. Not three people — one empty face and its reflections.

inspiration.title: Shop-window mannequins, split by the glass
inspiration.credit: a found photograph (kept local, not redistributed)
inspiration.mood: stylized soulless souls — our digitized image with the soul gone; the seam that is never closed

story.h: The structure is the signal chain
story.body: A warm analog phrase is forced through an ADC: anti-alias filter → sample/hold → quantize → and on the way back, a DAC reconstruction filter. Quantization error is irreversible — the bits below the least-significant bit are gone. That is the soul we lose.

The ADC is not a metaphor for aging; it is the same event. A continuous, infinitely-detailed thing — a sound, a youth, a self — forced through a finite gate, and what falls below the resolution cannot be rebuilt. The DAC reaching back is us, every time we remember who we were. It is lossy, so we keep going.

Under the whole converter loop runs a stardust bed at free pitches that is NEVER sent through the converter — so it does not quantize. When the soul is finally gridded to nothing, the dust is still shimmering. The form is lost; the matter is conserved. That is the consolation, and it is structural, not sentimental.

The soul sings in D minor; the mannequin answers in whole-tone (no leading tone, no possible cadence). The lead literally migrates from one scale to the other as it digitizes. The piece ends unresolved — the DAC reaches back at 3:34, almost becomes warm again, fails, and the loop restarts a notch lower.

ingredients.name: supersaw → phaser → soft_clip
ingredients.role: the analog soul: a phased D-minor string pad (the Jarre body)

ingredients.name: bitcrush(bits, rate)
ingredients.role: the converter: re-quantized harder each pass, 16→10→7→5→3→2 bit

ingredients.name: resonant_lpf (×2)
ingredients.role: anti-alias filter before sampling, reconstruction filter after (the DAC)

ingredients.name: additive — two leads
ingredients.role: lead_dm (D minor) and lead_wt (whole-tone), blended per pass so the melody migrates to the grid

ingredients.name: reflect() — Haas delay
ingredients.role: the glass reflection (Jarre's Revox trick: dry one side, delayed the other)

ingredients.name: seam_split()
ingredients.role: the split middle mannequin: bright in the left ear, dark in the right

ingredients.name: stardust twinkles
ingredients.role: the conserved matter — free pitches, never converted, shining through at the end

ingredients.name: sine pips + grid clicks
ingredients.role: the Ikeda/Noto glitch field — the aesthetics of error

transformations.feeling: the soul snapping to the grid
the DAC trying, and failing, to come back
what survives when the form is gone

editions.note: first render of the converter loop; stardust substrate; unresolved ending

path.text: Shares the mannequin photo: 'stylized soulless souls… we have digitized our lives and our image and forgotten our analog roots. We need to find the ingredients to make this recipe work.'
Names it Vitrine (the glass that shows you and traps you). Frames it as a real DSP dichotomy — analog warmth vs digital error — not a symbol.
Chooses the ending: 'they never resolve' — the duel held, like the seam in the middle mannequin.
Hands over the true structure: 'follow the AD conversion — start analog, lose quality each step (error), then DA tries to recapture what was lost… but it is lossy, so we keep going.'
'What the hardware does and what we do in life — we lose and try to regain, endlessly. People who lose their youth and try to reconstruct it. We are all made of stardust, as one great said.'
Web-searches the lineage (Basinski / Ikeda / Noto / Jarre); maps each technique onto an engine function; writes vitrine.py as the converter loop with a stardust substrate that does not quantize.

First render fails on a length mismatch (the pad's release grew the phrase); aligns lengths in source(); re-renders 300 s. Levels verified — loudest point is the DA reach-back at 3:34, exactly where it should be.

creations.title: The Black Theater
creations.tagline: A childhood dream: a child alone on a dark stage, watched by an audience of eyes.
creations.source_material: Pure synthesis — there is no recording. A childhood dream in three movements that follow the audience's eyes: I — kind; II — adoration & envy; III — the eyes close. A cosmic-horror, Lovecraftian mood.

story.body: I (0–30 s): kind, sparse, safe — a child paces onto a dark stage. II (30–66 s): the eyes swell with adoration, then sour into envy. III (66–96 s): the eyes close; the steps fade into the dark.

Each voice is a character: the pedal is the stage floor, the audience is the watching eyes, the child is a breathy lonely flute, the fishing is a sounding into the void, the stardust is cosmic glitter, the footsteps are uneven and human.

ingredients.name: additive / supersaw
ingredients.role: the organ pedal, the audience drone, the child's flute

ingredients.name: resonant_lpf + LFO
ingredients.role: dark, slowly-breathing drone filtering

ingredients.name: ping (struck pitch-drop)
ingredients.role: the fishing — a sounding into the void

ingredients.name: stardust
ingredients.role: analog cosmic glitter woven through all three movements

ingredients.name: reverb_st
ingredients.role: the cavernous theatre space

supercollider.name: \labvoice / \labadd
supercollider.role: the drones and the child, voiced live

supercollider.name: \labping
supercollider.role: the fishing sounding (replaced a naked held sine)

supercollider.name: \masterlimiter
supercollider.role: tail-of-tree limiter that ended the white-noise blow-ups

transformations.feeling: each organ described in words, designed by the model
keep the AI's color, never its glare

editions.note: re-voiced through SuperCollider via the full AI pipeline

path.text: A Lovecraftian, cosmic-horror mood — a child alone on a stage, watched. Asks to build the dream as music.
Builds dream.py: six organs, three movements, A Phrygian, automation lanes for the eyes.
Wants it re-voiced on a pro realtime engine — chooses SuperCollider, then the full AI pipeline to design the timbres.
'Was this noise? … it was like white noise … I think some patch had clipped.'
Adds a master limiter, a reciprocal-Q clamp, an FM index cap, and per-voice tanh — the blow-ups stop.
'Now it's like a mosquito.' / 'one note like a sine, intrusive.' / 'sounds out of concept, like melodica notes.'
Replaces the sustained bell with soft stardust; turns the naked sine into the struck fishing ping; makes the footsteps intentional and beautified.

creations.title: Icarus, or the Effort of Flight
creations.tagline: A flying dream fused with cosmic horror, built on the sound of Jupiter.
creations.source_material: A flying dream — clapping the hands fast to stay just above the rooftops; the feeling great, the effort hard to hold — built on a real recording of Jupiter's ion-acoustic plasma waves (space_library/jupiter_ion_acoustic.mp3, University of Iowa, CC BY 4.0).

story.body: Struggle (0–45 s): dissonant tension, fighting to rise. Breakthrough (~46 s): a held breath, then it bursts into bright Lydian joy — you're flying. Keep the pace: high but relentless, the altitude soaring yet wavering.

An 'effort' automation lane alternates flap (clap on, bass driving) and glide (clap off — you float, and wind rushes past). The lead floats on altitude, not effort, so it keeps singing even when the hands rest.

ingredients.name: load_audio (Jupiter)
ingredients.role: the source recording, decoded to the engine

ingredients.name: resample_speed(0.35)
ingredients.role: the slow, low abyss

ingredients.name: granular_cloud
ingredients.role: the shifting void cloud

ingredients.name: spectral_drone
ingredients.role: a sustained drone in the sample's color

ingredients.name: reverse + filter sweep
ingredients.role: the riser into the breakthrough

ingredients.name: stack + phaser
ingredients.role: the pad crossfading from dark to bright Lydian

ingredients.name: noise → bandpass
ingredients.role: the clap (the effort) and the rushing wind (the glide)

editions.note: the dramatic-arc edition (struggle → breakthrough → keep the pace)

path.text: 'Once I had a dream I could fly — clap my hands really fast, just above the tops of the houses. The feeling was great; the effort was hard to keep the altitude.'
Asks to build on the Jupiter ion-acoustic recording and to fuse the flight with cosmic horror.
Engineers the Jupiter sample four ways (abyss, granular cloud, spectral drone, reversed riser); builds the struggle→breakthrough→keep-the-pace arc with flap/glide effort lanes.

creations.title: Homage to Oxygène
creations.tagline: The Jarre signature, rebuilt from scratch in the real engine.
creations.source_material: The Jean-Michel Jarre signature itself — the spirit, not a sample. A study in where our sound comes from, rebuilt from first principles.

story.body: Before inventing, understand the lineage. This rebuilds the Oxygène palette — phased detuned strings, a bubbling arpeggio, a portamento lead — entirely in our own engine, so the techniques become ours to use.

ingredients.name: detuned saws → phaser
ingredients.role: the pads (the Eminent-310 through a Small Stone phaser)

ingredients.name: square arp + resonant_lpf
ingredients.role: the bubbling 16th-note motion

ingredients.name: saw + lpf, octave pattern
ingredients.role: the pulsing octave bass

ingredients.name: exponential glide + vibrato
ingredients.role: the singing portamento lead (the soul)

ingredients.name: sine/noise kit
ingredients.role: soft electronic kick, snare, hat

supercollider.name: \labglide
supercollider.role: the realtime equivalent of the portamento lead (Lag.kr glide, gated)

transformations.feeling: a lead that sings rather than steps

path.text: Asks where our sound comes from, and whether we can study the Jarre signature directly.
Rebuilds the Oxygène palette from scratch — phased strings, arp, octave bass, a portamento glide lead — so the techniques become part of the lab.
